title: Choosing between the housing regulator and the consumer forum
p1:
A delayed home handover does not always mean the same legal route will give you the best result.

Many homebuyers rush to file a complaint without knowing whether they should approach the housing regulator or the consumer forum.
p2:
The surprising part? Both forums can help, but they serve different purposes. The housing regulator mainly focuses on enforcing real estate rules, project compliance, and protecting buyers under housing laws. The consumer forum looks at unfair service, deficiency, and compensation claims.

Choosing the wrong path can affect the time, relief, and strategy available to you.
p3:
For example, if a builder delays possession or violates project obligations, the housing regulator may offer specific remedies under real estate laws. If the issue involves poor service or financial loss, a consumer complaint may be more suitable.

The best choice depends on what outcome you want: compliance, possession, refund, or compensation.
conclusion: Have you seen a homebuyer choose the wrong forum and lose valuable time? What factors do you think should decide between the housing regulator and the consumer forum?
